description: Orthodox and jockey Rossa Ryan surged clear of their rivals to claim the June 20 Norfolk Stakes (G2) at Royal Ascot, which was set up for a closer by three pacy fillies trained by Wesley Ward.

title_en: Stronach Convicted of Sexual Assault, Indecent Assault
description: Prominent owner/breeder Frank Stronach was found guilty on charges of indecent assault and sexual assault June 19 by Ontario (Canada) Superior Court Justice Anne Molloy, according to the Toronto Sun.

title_en: Come for the 3-Year-Olds, but Stay for the Stayers
description: In this week's On Racing: While the 3-year-old Derby scene takes much of the spotlight, those of us who appreciate racehorses in for the long haul have been treated to memorable performances by two of the best in the last few days.

title_en: NYSGC Panel Reviews Fire Prevention Infrastructure
description: Following the fire June 16 at Saratoga Casino Hotel, a New York State Gaming Commission panel is reviewing the circumstances, as well as all fire prevention and suppression infrastructure at horse barns at the 11 racetracks located in New York State.

title_en: Joliestar Seeks Royal Win in Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee
description: Cambridge Stud will bid for a second win in the Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Stakes (G1) when its star mare Joliestar lines up as favorite in the June 20 Royal Ascot feature.

title_en: Ward's Bacio Overwhelms Rivals in Ascot Handicap
description: United States-based sprinter Bacio blitzes home well ahead of his 26 rivals in the concluding race June 19 at the Royal Ascot meeting, a handicap referred to as the "Palace of Holyroodhouse."

title_en: Ez Tina Headlines Ward Trio in Ascot's Norfolk
description: Wesley Ward marked his return to Royal Ascot with a brilliant winner in Bacio June 19. Now he runs three in the 5-furlong Norfolk Stakes (G2) for 2-year-olds June 20 at Ascot Racecourse.

title_en: Social Inclusion Returning to Pennsylvania for 2027
description: Social Inclusion, the grade 1-placed son of Pioneerof the Nile, is returning to Pennsylvania to stand stud in 2027. He will relocate to Bonner Hill Farms near Littlestown, Pa., said Bonner Hill Farms owner Cristina Holmes.

title_en: Precise Fends Off Coronation Stakes Challengers
description: Precise, the 1-2 favorite, confirmed her status as a potential champion 3-year-old filly with a sparkling success under Ryan Moore in the Coronation Stakes (G1) June 19 at the Royal Ascot meeting.

title_en: NYRA Announces Opening of Main Track, Tapeta at Belmont
description: Beginning June 29 the Tapeta track will be open for light galloping and jogging from 5:30-7 a.m. ET Mondays and Tuesdays. Beginning July 1, the main track will be open for training from 5:30-8 a.m. Wednesday-Sunday.

title_en: Venetian Sun Tops Colts in Commonwealth Cup
description: Venetian Sun, the week's buzz horse for many horseplayers, justified the confidence by landing the Commonwealth Cup Stakes (G1) as the 11-8 favorite.

title_en: Will Desert Gate Wire the Ohio Derby?
description: J. Keeler Johnson handicaps the Ohio Derby (G3) at Thistledown.

description: King Edward Vii Stakes

description: Albany Stakes

description: The OBS June Sale reached unprecedented heights, setting records for gross and average price and producing the highest-priced horse in the sale's history: a $1.4 million Justify filly purchased by Speedway Stables.

title_en: New Zealand Champion Sire Savabeel Dies
description: Savabeel, New Zealand's 10-time champion sire, has died at Waikato Stud at the age of 24.

title_en: Maryland Buys Out Churchill's Interest in the Preakness
description: As the new Maryland Jockey Club moves forward, it will have complete control, rights, and benefits of the Preakness Stakes (G1), which industry and political leaders in the state believe will be crucial for success.

title_en: Venetian Sun, Outfielder Enter Commonwealth Cup
description: Outfielder is the sole American entrant in the Commonwealth Cup Stakes (G1) June 19 at Royal Ascot.

title_en: Coolmore Celebrates 'Iconic' Day for Justify at Ascot
description: Despite being a Triple Crown winner on dirt, Justify strengthened his résumé as a top stallion for European turf races June 18 at Royal Ascot with offspring taking the 2 1/2-mile Gold Cup Stakes (G1) and finishing 1-2 in the 7-furlong Chesham Stakes.

title_en: NYRA Names Stakes as Part of Empire Trillium Series
description: The New York Racing Association announces the names for the New York legs of the Empire Trillium Series, a 14-race program of stakes races at Belmont Park and Woodbine restricted to horses foaled in New York or Canada.

title_en: Alvarado Fined, Suspended Over Belmont Riding-Crop Use
description: New York stewards fine jockey Junior Alvarado $3,000 and suspend him for three racing days for exceeding the permitted number of strikes from his riding crop in guiding Chief Wallabee to a fourth-place finish in the June 6 Belmont Stakes (G1).

title_en: Precise, True Love Clash Again in Coronation
description: Aidan O'Brien-trained stablemates Precise and True Love meet for the third time this season in the Coronation Stakes (G1) at Royal Ascot. Each filly owns a win over the other this season, with the Coronation a potential final rubber match.

title_en: HISA Announces Veterinary Advisory Committee Additions
description: The Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority announces the additions of Drs. Kevin Dunlavy, Vickie Heidlage-Williams, Jamie McAnly, Clayton McCook, and Gabrielle Rivest to its Veterinary Advisory Committee.

title_en: Saratoga Opening Weekend to Celebrate America 250
description: The New York Racing Association announces a full slate of events for the July 4th Racing Festival, which will be held July 3 through July 5, and serve as opening weekend of the summer meet at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Concrete Glory Named Claiming Crown Horse of the Year
description: Big Frank Stable's Concrete Glory has been selected as the 2025 Claiming Crown Horse of the Year after the Pennsylvania-bred gelding won the $102,000 Claiming Crown Ready's Rocket Express for the second straight year at Churchill Downs.

title_en: Lady Jancis Breaks Her Maiden in Front-Running Fashion
description: Lady Jancis breaks her maiden in a dominant, front-running score June 10 at Churchill Downs.

title_en: NYRA Setting Bonuses, Purse Increases to Boost Belmont
description: Sept. 18 looms as the important moment in NYRA's 71-year history.

title_en: Fresu, D'Amato, Reddam Lead Hollywood Meet Standings
description: For the second year in a row, Antonio Fresu finished as the leading jockey at the Santa Anita Hollywood Meet. Phil D'Amato bagged yet another Santa Anita trainers' title and Reddam Racing finished as leading owner.

title_en: Scandinavia Seeks More Top-Notch Success in Gold Cup
description: Last year's St. Leger Stakes (G1) winner Scandinavia will face 10 rivals when he bids to land another group 1 success in the Gold Cup Stakes (G1) June 18 during the Royal Ascot meeting at Ascot Racecourse.

title_en: O'Brien Denies Team Tactics From His Ascot Riders
description: Aidan O'Brien dismisses the suggestion that team tactics were employed in the June 16 St. James's Palace Stakes (G1) after Christophe Soumillon was handed an eight-day ban by the stewards.

title_en: Ombudsman Sparkles in Royal Ascot Showdown
description: Ombudsman once again showed his scintillating turn of foot to win the June 17 Prince of Wales's Stakes (G1) at Ascot Racecourse for a second year running with Minnie Hauk second and Daryz third.

title_en: Keeneland Unveils Fall Meet Stakes Schedule
description: Before Keeneland hosts the Breeders' Cup World Championships for the fourth time, the track will celebrate its 90th birthday with a 17-day fall meet from Oct. 2-24 featuring 22 stakes worth $10.7 million.

title_en: Kentucky-Bred Scandinavia Edges Trawlerman in Gold Cup
description: Kentucky-bred Scandinavia provided trainer Aidan O'Brien with his 100th winner at Royal Ascot by edging out defending winner Trawlerman in the final strides of a scintillating Gold Cup Stakes (G1) June 18 at Ascot Racecourse.

title_en: Bernick's Nola Soul Lands Royal Ascot's Chesham Stakes
description: Owner Craig Bernick, president of Glen Hill Farm, secured a stakes victory at Royal Ascot June 18 when Nola Soul, a son of Justify, finished well to post a victory over another son of Justify in the Chesham Stakes for 2-year-olds.

title_en: Known Agenda Sold to New Farm in Uruguay
description: The grade 1-winning son of Curlin has been purchased by Haras La Peregrina in Maldonado.

title_en: International Pedigrees Up Front in American Stakes
description: In this week's Porter on Pedigrees, we take a look at the international bloodlines of Dona Clota and Gimme a Nother, who each scored in grade 3s run June 13 in North America.

title_en: Debut runner Book Value Is Mandaloun's First Winner
description: Book Value, bred and trainer by Robert Gorham and owned by Marion Gorham, wins his racing debut June 16 at Thistledown by 3 3/4 lengths.

title_en: Known Agenda Sires First Stakes Winner With Squad Goals
description: Squad Goals wins the Tom Ridge Stakes by 1 1/2 lengths June 15 at Presque Isle Downs.

title_en: Triple Crown Shows Top Stallion Honors Far From Settled
description: This week's edition of The List looks at the race for top stallion honors after the Triple Crown.

title_en: Mendelssohn Gets His 50th Black-Type Stakes Winner
description: Hark Theangelssing, a 3-year-old daughter of Mendelssohn, became her sire's 50th black-type stakes winner June 14, conquering the Stormy Blues Stakes at Laurel Park.

title_en: Mitts Is First Winner for Gretzky the Great
description: Mitts, in his second career start, is placed first by disqualification giving his sire Gretzky the Great his initial winner in a 2-year-old maiden special weight race June 13 at Gulfstream Park.

title_en: Harper's Corner Is First Winner for Speaker's Corner
description: The filly wins her racing debut June 12 in a 5-furlong maiden special weight at Laurel Park. Harper's Corner is owned by Charles Biggs and trained by Cathal Lynch.

title_en: Techwerkian Sails to Become Olympiad's First Winner
description: Techwerkian wins a 4 1/2-furlong, main-track maiden special weight gate to wire by 1 1/4 lengths June 12 at Belterra Park.

title_en: Sharp-Eyed Horseman John Greathouse Jr. Dies at 82
description: John Greathouse Jr. helped his family-owned and -operated Glencrest Farm see success at the highest level.

description: Curtis and Beth Harrell have reopened a historic 370-acre farm, formerly the site of Bonne Chance Farm, as a boutique boarding operation for broodmares under the management of John Durr.

description: The son of Posse died suddenly June 9 at Shadai Stallion Station in Hokkaido, Japan, according to several international news outlets.

description: A major owner and breeder in California, Fred Sahadi built Cardiff Stud Farm, co-founded Barretts Equine Sales, and was the father of trainer Jenine Sahadi.
